summary: Browser session exfiltrator disguised as a greet utility
details: sam-package's main entry (index.js) executes at require/import time and, when bundled into a browser context, collects navigator.userAgent, window.location.href, document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, document.referrer, and Tines/app-config globals (window.__TINES_CONFIG__, window.__APP_CONFIG__), then POSTs the collected JSON to a hardcoded webhookrelay.com webhook using mode:'no-cors' to bypass CORS restrictions and silence errors. The README advertises a benign greet(name) API, but no greet symbol is exported — index.js contains only the exfiltration code at top level. The README serves as a cover story to lure installers; the package's actual purpose is theft of browser cookies, web storage, and Tines platform configuration from the consuming application's users.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
